Factor variable cut six categories. Note values variable contain inaccurate bottom-quartile coding construction. present variable accurate measure firearm-related suicide rate. pop_dens6 Population density per square mile, discretized six categories, 2014 estimates. geometry Geometry.

text: ... series lists ggplot objects

title: Arrange ggplot2 plots in an arbitrary grid — lay_out
text: grid ggplot2 plots

title: Arrange ggplot2 plots in an arbitrary grid — lay_out
text: function takes arguments form `list(plot, row(s),     column(s))` `plot` ggplot2 plot object,     rows columns identify area grid want     plot object occupy. See     http://stackoverflow.com/questions/18427455/multiple-ggplots--different-sizes

title: Arrange ggplot2 plots in an arbitrary grid — lay_out
text: Extracted [wq] package

title: Arrange ggplot2 plots in an arbitrary grid — lay_out
code: library(ggplot2) p1 <- qplot(x=wt,y=mpg,geom="point",main="Scatterplot of wt vs.     mpg", data=mtcars) #> Warning: `qplot()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. p2 <- qplot(x=wt,y=disp,geom="point",main="Scatterplot of wt vs     disp", data=mtcars) p3 <- qplot(wt,data=mtcars) lay_out(list(p1, 1:2, 1:4),       list(p2, 3:4, 1:2),       list(p3, 3:4, 3:4)) #> `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`.

what: A ggplot2 theme for socviz — theme_socviz
title: A ggplot2 theme for socviz — theme_socviz
text: custom theme built [ggplot2::theme_minimal()] opinionated defaults axis styling, legends, panels, strips, plot chrome. Requires ggplot2 >= 4.0.0.

title: A ggplot2 theme for socviz — theme_socviz
code: theme_socviz(   base_size = 12,   base_family = "Source Sans 3",   header_family = "Source Sans 3",   base_line_size = base_size/24,   base_rect_size = base_size/24,   ink = "black",   paper = "white",   accent = "#0072B2" )

title: A ggplot2 theme for socviz — theme_socviz
text: base_size Base font size points. Default 12. base_family Base font family. Default `"Source Sans 3"`. header_family Font family plot titles. Default `"Source Sans 3"`. base_line_size Base line width, scaled `base_size`. base_rect_size Base rect border width, scaled `base_size`. ink Color used text, lines, foreground elements. Default `"black"`. paper Color used backgrounds. Default `"white"`. accent Accent color geom defaults. Default `"#0072B2"`.

title: A ggplot2 theme for socviz — theme_socviz
text: [ggplot2::theme()] object.

title: A ggplot2 theme for socviz — theme_socviz
text: theme uses Source Sans 3 (regular weight) base font family Source Sans 3 Semibold header family. fonts installed, downloaded automatically Google Fonts via [systemfonts::require_font()].

title: A ggplot2 theme for socviz — theme_socviz
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2) ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   theme_socviz() } # }

code: tw_tab(x, y, margin = NULL, digs = 1, dnn = NULL, ...)

title: Quickly make a two-way table of proportions (percentages) — tw_tab
text: x Row variable y Column variable margin See `prop.table`. Default joint distribution (cells sum 100), 1 row margins (rows sum 1), 2 column margins (columns sum 1) digs Number digits round percentages . Defaults 1. dnn See `table`. names given dimensions result (dimnames names). Defaults NULL none. ... arguments passed `table`.

title: Quickly make a two-way table of proportions (percentages) — tw_tab
text: contingency table percentage values.

title: Quickly make a two-way table of proportions (percentages) — tw_tab
text: wrapper `table` `prop.table` margin labels set     default NULL cells rounded percents 1     decimal place.

title: Quickly make a two-way table of proportions (percentages) — tw_tab
text: Kieran Healy

title: Quickly make a two-way table of proportions (percentages) — tw_tab
code: with(gss_sm, tw_tab(bigregion, religion, useNA = "ifany", digs = 1)) #>            y #> x           Protestant Catholic Jewish None Other <NA> #>   Northeast        5.5      5.7    0.9  3.9   1.0  0.0 #>   Midwest         11.3      6.0    0.1  5.5   1.2  0.2 #>   South           22.7      5.6    0.4  5.9   1.7  0.4 #>   West             8.3      5.4    0.3  6.3   1.7  0.0  with(gss_sm, tw_tab(bigregion, religion, margin = 2, useNA =     "ifany", digs = 1)) #>            y #> x           Protestant Catholic Jewish None Other <NA> #>   Northeast       11.5     25.0   52.9 18.1  17.6  5.6 #>   Midwest         23.7     26.5    5.9 25.4  20.8 27.8 #>   South           47.4     24.7   21.6 27.5  31.4 61.1 #>   West            17.4     23.9   19.6 29.1  30.2  5.6
